name: Disaster Recovery Setup
description: Design and implement disaster recovery capabilities including backup strategies, failover mechanisms, and recovery testing.

inputs.name: critical_services
inputs.description: List of services, databases, and data stores requiring disaster recovery protection.

inputs.name: rto_rpo_targets
inputs.description: Recovery Time Objective and Recovery Point Objective for each critical service.

inputs.name: infrastructure_topology
inputs.description: Current deployment topology including regions, availability zones, and dependencies.

inputs.name: compliance_requirements
inputs.description: Regulatory requirements for data residency, encryption, and retention.

outputs.name: dr_architecture
outputs.description: Disaster recovery architecture with primary, secondary, and failover configurations.

outputs.name: backup_strategy
outputs.description: Backup schedules, retention policies, encryption, and storage locations.

outputs.name: failover_runbook
outputs.description: Step-by-step failover procedure with automated and manual steps.

outputs.name: recovery_test_report
outputs.description: Results from DR test execution including RTO/RPO achievement and gaps.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is DR capability design, not active incident response or monitoring setup.
Require defined RTO/RPO targets before provisioning backup or replication infrastructure.
Validate each backup or replication component produces verified artifacts before proceeding.
Return DR architecture, backup strategy, failover runbook, and recovery test report.

steps.description: Analyze critical services, define RTO/RPO targets, and identify compliance constraints.
Design multi-region or multi-cloud DR topology with primary, warm standby, and failover paths.
Configure automated backups with encryption, retention policies, and off-site storage.
Enable cross-region data replication for databases and stateful services with consistency guarantees.
Create automated failover scripts, DNS switching, and health-check-driven failover triggers.
Run a full DR test simulating a regional failure and measure RTO/RPO achievement.
Document gaps from DR test, create remediation plan, and update runbooks.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read infrastructure topology, compliance requirements, and existing backup configurations.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write DR architecture docs, backup configs, failover scripts, and test reports.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.purpose: Execute backup commands, replication setup, and failover test scripts.

verification.method: Backup restoration succeeds for each critical service within RPO target.
Cross-region replication lag is within acceptable bounds for all data stores.
Automated failover completes within RTO target during DR test.
Backup encryption and data residency meet all defined compliance requirements.

success_criteria: DR architecture covers all critical services with defined RTO/RPO.
Backups are automated, encrypted, and restoration-tested.
Failover mechanism is automated and tested end-to-end.
DR test report confirms RTO/RPO targets are met.

rollback.when: If DR test reveals data loss or extended RTO, revert to primary and remediate gaps before re-testing.
